arc_method_mapping.classification_of_becoming: classify each source row at the lowest supported rung; separate internal support state, external resource state, external perturbation state, external structured state, boundary-crossing trace, readiness-only context, and blocked relabels
arc_method_mapping.cultivation_of_becoming: cultivate reusable source-current boundary traces and fail-closed controls before optimizing any local AP6 label
arc_method_mapping.interrogation_of_becoming: treat B-axis and C-axis records as bounded questions about boundary separability, not proof of selfhood or identity acceptance
arc_method_mapping.naturalization_of_becoming: keep artifact-level boundary prerequisites separate from native support and native self/environment understanding

constructed_support: use B0 as active null for coherent-looking outside structure
inherited_closed_claims: structured external coherence cannot be promoted without source-backed boundary side assignment
boundary_state_lineage.name: null / external coherence only
required_N16_evidence: B0 x C3 must reject structured external coherence as self-boundary

constructed_support: inside/outside partition and boundary edges can be proposed from pinned surfaces
inherited_closed_claims: artifact-visible basin, sink, parent, and edge/flux surfaces exist
localized boundary coupling can be measured in fixtures
boundary_state_lineage.name: localized basin partition
required_N16_evidence: quiet B1 row must extract boundary edges without supplied labels
B1 x C2 must expose failure threshold under flux
unsupported_extension: boundary coupling is not AP6 boundary stability

constructed_support: B2 can be calibrated as a support-persistent boundary candidate under C0-C2 before repair claims
inherited_closed_claims: support-retention and support-seeking regulation are source-backed at AP3 artifact level
N07 source-specific support/basin compatibility replays artifact-only
N15 drift/replay discipline can preserve derived artifact target context
boundary_state_lineage.name: support-persistent basin
required_N16_evidence: B2 x C0, C1, and C2 must be evaluated before B3 is unlocked
boundary-side assignments must be replayable and source-current
unsupported_extension: artifact replay stability is not native self/environment understanding

constructed_support: B3 can test whether a boundary breach is repaired or fail-closed without post-hoc relabeling
inherited_closed_claims: bounded regulation and support restoration contexts exist at artifact level
explicit support withdrawal/restoration baselines are source-backed
boundary_state_lineage.name: regulated repair / reabsorption boundary
required_N16_evidence: B3 remains locked until B2 C0-C2 evaluations are present or explicitly blocked
B3 x C4 must distinguish reclosure from relabeling
unsupported_extension: bounded correction is not native boundary repair
explicit restoration is not autonomous reabsorption

constructed_support: B4 can be included as the weakest candidate state for shared-medium separability
inherited_closed_claims: route alternatives, constructed consequence followout, and dual-basin exchange analogs exist
N07 records source-specific dual-basin separability under bounded exchange
boundary_state_lineage.name: coupled multi-basin separability candidate
required_N16_evidence: B4 x C5 must test shared-medium multi-basin exclusivity
B4 x C2 must remain partial or not_applicable if shared substrate support is insufficient
unsupported_extension: prior route alternatives are not multi-basin boundary exclusivity
B4 x C2 is only a flux stress row

challenge_class_records.name: quiet reference
challenge_class_records.operational_role: calibration condition with no intended boundary stress

challenge_class_records.name: unstructured perturbation
challenge_class_records.operational_role: random or noisy disturbance around or across the boundary

challenge_class_records.name: directional flux
challenge_class_records.operational_role: one-sided pressure, drift, or flow across the boundary

challenge_class_records.name: structured external coherence
challenge_class_records.operational_role: active null for coherent outside pattern that must not be mistaken for self-boundary

challenge_class_records.name: breach and repair
challenge_class_records.operational_role: local boundary disruption followed by reclosure, reabsorption, or fail-closed classification

challenge_class_records.name: coupled neighbor / shared medium
challenge_class_records.operational_role: more than one candidate basin interacting through a shared substrate

direct_historic_ap6_support.interpretation: No pinned historic source directly supports AP6 internal/external state separability. Prior records provide B-axis lineage, challenge analogs, controls, and old-best AP3-AP5 construction inputs only.

iteration_interpretation.next_required_step: Freeze the N16 boundary schema, B-axis lineage contract, C-axis challenge-class contract, row decisions, source roles, budget surface, replay digest, and AP6 gates.
iteration_interpretation.plain_language_interpretation: Iteration 1 pins N16's evidence base. It finds no direct historic AP6 support, but records old-best AP3-AP5 inputs, B-axis lineage from prior basin/boundary examples, and operational C-axis challenge classes for later schema work.
iteration_interpretation.supported_interpretation: N16 has sufficient pinned source coverage to proceed to schema freeze. AP6 remains unassigned; the strongest path is constructed from old-best claims plus newly generated N16 boundary rows.

old_best_claim_inputs.required_N16_addition: separate internal support-relevant state from external resource, perturbation, or structured state under AP6 controls and replay
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_does_not_support: internal/external boundary-side assignment, semantic goal ownership, agency, identity acceptance, or native support
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_supports: artifact-level target/proxy condition generated from source-current support, memory, regulation, and AP4 context
old_best_claim_inputs.why_included: strongest closed AP prerequisite before N16; supplies source-current target/proxy formation discipline

old_best_claim_inputs.required_N16_addition: boundary-side descriptors that keep route/resource context separate from internal support state
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_does_not_support: intention, semantic choice, agency, or observed upstream route-conditioned support/regulation
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_supports: artifact-level consequence-sensitive route selection and constructed route-conditioned support/regulation followout
old_best_claim_inputs.why_included: supplies the strongest closed route/consequence context for external resource and directional-flux analogs

old_best_claim_inputs.required_N16_addition: source-current boundary rows that distinguish maintained internal support from external challenge state
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_does_not_support: selfhood, agency, identity acceptance, native support, or AP6 boundary-side separability
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_supports: artifact-level support-seeking regulation and support disruption/restoration stress context
old_best_claim_inputs.why_included: supplies the strongest closed support-maintenance and bounded response context for B2/B3

old_best_claim_inputs.required_N16_addition: explicit shared-medium boundary rows if memory alternatives are used for B4
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_does_not_support: multi-basin separability, identity acceptance, native memory support, or AP6 boundary evidence
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_supports: artifact-only route memory, trail/affordance context, and geometry-mediated alternatives
old_best_claim_inputs.why_included: supplies route-memory context for old-best construction and weak B4 shared-medium analogs

old_best_claim_inputs.required_N16_addition: breach/reclosure rows that distinguish repair from explicit support restoration or post-hoc relabeling
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_does_not_support: endogenous boundary repair, semantic goal ownership, native regulation, or identity support outcome
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_supports: artifact-level bounded proxy regulation and perturbation recovery context
old_best_claim_inputs.why_included: supplies perturbation/recovery and bounded-regulation context for B3 and C1/C4

old_best_claim_inputs.required_N16_addition: keep readiness weight/context separate from boundary-side evidence unless a separate Phase 8 task is opened
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_does_not_support: native support, AP6 boundary evidence, or fully native integration
old_best_claim_inputs.what_it_supports: readiness-only context for later native-policy work
old_best_claim_inputs.why_included: records Phase 8 readiness constraints and prevents readiness from being relabeled as native support
